days.label: Day 1
days.title: Arrival & Settling In
days.note: Land, get the scooty sorted, check in, and keep the rest of the day loose.

items.title: Arrive Sivagiri Station
items.subtitle: Pick up scooty — LocoRides or Unnikannan
items.why: Call ahead that morning so a scooter is actually held for you.

items.title: Check in & freshen up
items.subtitle: The Cliff Boutique Hotel, North Cliff

options.title: Chimney Family Restaurant
options.subtitle: Tawa chicken fry, chicken biryani
options.why: A relaxed re-entry meal — generous portions, easy parking, away from the cliff crowds for your first hour in town.

options.title: Sunset Multicuisine Restaurant
options.subtitle: Chicken multicuisine platter
options.why: Same easy-going first-meal energy, with a sea view if you'd rather have it right away.
options.location: Sunset Multicuisine Restaurant, North Cliff, Varkala

items.title: Open afternoon
items.subtitle: Cliff walk & Tibetan market
items.why: No agenda on purpose — walk the North Cliff path, browse stalls, let the train journey settle. Duck into any café if it drizzles.

options.title: Cafe del Mar
options.subtitle: Kerala Kozhi Chicken Roast with parotta, Alla Arrabbiata pasta
options.why: One of the most-loved sunset perches on the whole cliff — golden hour over the Arabian Sea with proper Italian cooking.
options.location: Cafe del Mar, North Cliff, Varkala

options.title: Cinnamon
options.subtitle: Chicken tikka, cocktail
options.why: A vibrant cliff-edge café-bar with the same ocean-view sunset appeal, livelier once the sun is down.

days.label: Day 2
days.title: North Side Beaches
days.note: Black Beach → Manthra → Odayam → Edava Vettakkada → Edava → Kappil. Six beaches — an early start keeps it unhurried instead of rushed.

options.title: Juicy Cafe
options.subtitle: Appam with vegetable stew, pineapple juice
options.why: Authentic Kerala-style breakfast at honest prices — good fuel before a long beach-hopping day.
options.location: Juicy Cafe, North Middle Cliff, Varkala

options.title: Bindu's Cafe
options.subtitle: Banana pancake, filter coffee
options.why: A cliff regular for coffee and sunrise-side views — nice change of pace if Juicy Cafe is busy.

options.title: Clafouti
options.subtitle: Aloo paratha, full breakfast spread
options.why: A long-standing North Cliff favourite for breakfast, right near Black Beach where the day starts.

items.title: Black Beach
items.subtitle: First stop, ~5–10 min ride
items.why: Quiet and rockier, far fewer crowds than the main beach — a nice first pause for photos and a barefoot walk.

items.title: Manthra Beach
items.subtitle: Hidden cove, ~10 min further
items.why: A tiny, rarely-visited cove tucked between Black Beach and Odayam — a quick stop rather than a long linger, but worth it for how empty it usually is.

items.title: Odayam Beach
items.subtitle: Twin rocks, ~3 km further north
items.why: Your first real hang of the day — calmer, more secluded shoreline, good for just sitting on the rocks and watching fishing boats.

items.title: Edava Vettakkada Beach
items.subtitle: Quiet cove near the lake estuary
items.why: Less developed than its neighbours — a brief stop mainly for the sight of the lake water meeting the sea, especially dramatic in monsoon season.

items.title: Edava Beach
items.subtitle: ~2 km further, backwater meets sea
items.why: One of the more unspoiled stretches in North Varkala — the spot where the backwater channel almost touches the ocean, very photogenic.

options.title: InDa Cafe
options.subtitle: Grilled chicken with garlic rice, lemon mint cooler
options.why: Tucked away from the main cliff crowd on the Odayam road — peaceful, homemade-style food, a good reset before the longer stretch north.

options.title: Oottupura Vegetarian Restaurant
options.subtitle: Kerala-style vegetarian thali
options.why: A well-loved pure-veg option on the same road, if you want a lighter midday meal before more riding.

items.title: Arrive Kappil Beach & Lake
items.subtitle: The standout of the day
items.why: A narrow road runs between the sea on one side and the backwater lake on the other, with a bridge across the lake for photos.

items.title: Kappil Lake kayaking
items.subtitle: Calm estuary paddling
items.why: Beginner-friendly, coconut groves either side, good odds of spotting kingfishers and egrets. July is monsoon season — water can run rougher or murkier, so decide with the operator on the day rather than pre-booking. If it's paused, the bridge walk alone is worth it.

options.title: Little Tibet
options.subtitle: Spinach-corn momos, chicken thukpa, banana shake
options.why: Beautiful sea views, calm cliff-edge breeze, and reliably good Tibetan food to end a long beach day.

options.title: Darjeeling Cafe
options.subtitle: Chicken sizzler
options.why: A global-menu cliff café with the same easy sea-view wind-down, if Little Tibet is full.

items.title: Optional: Sandy Bar nightlife
items.subtitle: Black Beach — Varkala's best-known party spot
items.why: If you're up for a late one after dinner, Sandy Bay Cafe at Black Beach (where you started the day) is the go-to for music and a livelier scene. Entirely optional — skip it if the beach day has worn you out.

days.label: Day 3
days.title: Jatayu Earth's Center + South Beaches
days.note: Inland in the morning, back to the coast for sunset — the day is sequenced so you're not backtracking.

options.title: ABBA Bakery Café
options.subtitle: Brick-oven bread & eggs, cappuccino
options.why: Quick, simple fuel since there's a 45–60 minute ride ahead.

options.title: Coffee Temple
options.subtitle: Croissant, cappuccino
options.why: A charming bakery alternative right on the cliff if ABBA is busy.

items.title: Ride to Jatayu Earth's Center
items.subtitle: Chadayamangalam, ~27–28 km
items.why: Fill the tank before leaving Varkala town �� fewer petrol stops on this stretch.

items.title: Jatayu Earth's Center
items.subtitle: Cable car, museum, 6D theatre
items.why: Entry ~₹400/person, cash only — no cards or UPI at the counter. Take the cable car up rather than the 826-step climb in July humidity.

options.title: True Thomas
options.subtitle: Kerala-style chicken curry with parotta
options.why: A well-loved local favourite blending North and South Indian food — a hearty stop on the ride back into town.

options.title: Ruchi Restaurant
options.subtitle: Chicken curry rice
options.why: Another solid Kerala-cuisine stop closer to the helipad road if timing works out better.

items.title: South Cliff & Papanasam Beach walk
items.subtitle: Quieter, less commercial stretch
items.why: An unhurried pre-sunset stroll before dinner.

options.title: Cafe Trip is Life
options.subtitle: Wood-fired chicken pizza, pineapple-ginger cooler
options.why: One of the best sea-facing perches on the south cliff — arrive an hour before sunset for a front-row table.
options.location: Cafe Trip is Life, South Cliff, Varkala

options.title: Soul Cafe
options.subtitle: Chicken sandwich, pineapple ginger juice
options.why: Unobstructed sea views and a properly chill vibe, right nearby on the same cliff.

options.title: Tibetan Kitchen
options.subtitle: Momos, thukpa, garlic fish
options.why: Right on Papanasam Cliff where you'll already be for the sunset walk — reliably good Tibetan food with a first-floor sea view.

options.title: Cafe Sarwaa
options.subtitle: Smoothie bowl, beef curry combo with porotta
options.why: One of the most photographed sunset spots on the south cliff, with steps straight down to a quiet private beach.

days.label: Day 4
days.title: Easy Morning & Departure
days.note: Everything today stays close to the hotel — lunch now starts at 12 (not 11:30) so mealtimes aren't back-to-back with breakfast; wrapped by around 3:10 PM for the 3:40 PM train.

options.title: Sun Francisco Cafe
options.subtitle: French toast / pancake stack, filter coffee
options.why: Consistently rated one of the best cafés in Varkala — a calm, unrushed final breakfast with excellent sea views.

options.title: Lumuma
options.subtitle: Acai bowl, coffee
options.why: The only vegan café on the North Cliff, if you want something lighter on the last day.

items.title: Varkala / Papanasam beach walk
items.subtitle: Right below the cliff steps
items.why: Ticks off the last beach without needing the scooty at all — good last window for souvenir shopping too.

options.title: Trattorias
options.subtitle: Chicken pizza or chicken alfredo pasta
options.why: A fitting last cliff-view meal, close to the helipad road for an easy ride back.

options.title: Cafe Italiano
options.subtitle: Chicken pizza
options.why: Laid-back Italian spot nearby with the same easy vibe if Trattorias is packed.

options.title: Cafe Iyra
options.subtitle: Wood-fired pizza, lunch bowls
options.why: Another well-regarded North Cliff lunch option if you'd rather stay put than head toward Trattorias.
